answer:
**In Allama Iqbal's poem "Har nafas Iqbal tera aah mein mastoor hai" (Bang-e-Dara, Part 3, Chapter 10), the poem is framed as "The Muslim" (مسلم)—Iqbal's inner, assertive Muslim self—addressing and rebuking Iqbal himself for dwelling in lamentation and despair over the past.[^1]**

The opening lines directly criticize Iqbal's sighs and nostalgia, portraying his heart as filled with cries rather than hope, deaf to the present, and spreading despair like a "bell of a slumbering caravan." It urges him: "O bell of a slumbering caravan, be silent! / Your voice is a breeder of despair, be silent!" The self declares that the bygone era cannot return.[^2]

The Muslim self then speaks powerfully in first person (starting "Companion! I am a Muslim"), affirming its eternal role as bearer of *Tauheed* (Oneness of God), witness to truth since eternity, source of vitality in existence, protector of Being, and radiant star of the world's destiny. It rejects despair, proclaiming faith in the Muslim community's future victory: "How can this fleeting spectacle of sorrow frighten me? / I have faith in the destiny of my Community. / My world is free from the element of despair; / The battle's righteous fervor proclaims a perfect victory."[^3]

The self admits gazing at the past but sees it as elixir for renewal, mirroring tomorrow in yesterday, foreseeing rebirth of prostration-born beauty and storm-splendors.[^4]

**Full chapter for context:** [^5]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Ch. 10 "Har nafas Iqbal tera aah mein mastoor hai" [translation](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/10-har-nafas-iqbal-tera-aah-mein-mastoor-hai/translation/#p-8cbcd5).
[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Ch. 10 [paragraphs](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/10-har-nafas-iqbal-tera-aah-mein-mastoor-hai/translation/?paragraphs=%5B%22p-489986%22,%22p-c56fbc%22,%22p-ae2c4e%22,%22p-6e170e%22,%22p-35599f%22,%22p-7db576%22).
[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Ch. 10 [paragraphs](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/10-har-nafas-iqbal-tera-aah-mein-mastoor-hai/translation/?paragraphs=%5B%22p-6ef7a5%22,%22p-65ad49%22,%22p-fcaf83%22,%22p-123d39%22,%22p-658960%22,%22p-ff38e6%22,%22p-7ba1e4%22,%22p-be7f82%22,%22p-c6f6a3%22).
[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Ch. 10 [paragraphs](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/10-har-nafas-iqbal-tera-aah-mein-mastoor-hai/translation/?paragraphs=%5B%22p-7c3704%22,%22p-75064e%22,%22p-8b95bb%22,%22p-ed9417%22,%22p-81acca%22).
[^5]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Ch. 10 [translation](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/10-har-nafas-iqbal-tera-aah-mein-mastoor-hai/translation/).

args.query: "Har Nafas Iqbal Tera Aah Mein Mastoor Hai"
